006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

010 *

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.markdown;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.util.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
026
027/**
028 * Session object for {@link MarkdownDocParser}.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * Parses Markdown documents (headings + tables) produced by {@link MarkdownDocSerializer}
032 * into Java objects by mapping heading sections to nested bean properties.
033 *
034 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
035 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
036 * </ul>
037 *
038 */
039@SuppressWarnings({
040   "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable for parser session hierarchy
041   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
042   "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for doParse / parseSections
043   "java:S6541", // Brain method acceptable for parseSections
044   "unchecked",
045   "rawtypes",
046})
047public class MarkdownDocParserSession extends MarkdownParserSession {
048
049   private static final String CONST_Property = "Property";
050   private static final String CONST_Value = "Value";
051
052   private final int headingLevel;
053
054   /**
055    * Builder class.
056    */
057   @SuppressWarnings({
058      "java:S110" // Inheritance depth acceptable for parser session builder hierarchy
059   })
060   public static class Builder extends MarkdownParserSession.Builder<Builder> {
061
062      int headingLevel;
063
064      /**
065       * Constructor.
066       *
067       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
068       */
069      protected Builder(MarkdownDocParser ctx) {
070         super(ctx);
071         headingLevel = ctx.headingLevel;
072      }
073
074      @Override
075      public MarkdownDocParserSession build() {
076         return new MarkdownDocParserSession(this);
077      }
078
079   }
080
081   /**
082    * Creates a new builder for this object.
083    *
084    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
085    * @return A new builder.
086    */
087   public static Builder create(MarkdownDocParser ctx) {
088      return new Builder(ctx);
089   }
090
091   /**
092    * Constructor.
093    *
094    * @param builder The builder for this object.
095    */
096   protected MarkdownDocParserSession(Builder builder) {
097      super(builder);
098      headingLevel = builder.headingLevel;
099   }
100
101   @Override /* Overridden from MarkdownParserSession */
102   protected <T> T doParse(ParserPipe pipe, ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
103      try (var r = new java.io.BufferedReader(pipe.getReader())) {
104         var lines = readAllLines(r);
105         return parseDocAnything(lines, type, getOuter(), headingLevel);
106      }
107   }
108
109   /**
110    * Main document-mode parse dispatch.
111    *
112    * @param <T> The target type.
113    * @param lines All input lines.
114    * @param eType The expected type.
115    * @param outer The outer object.
116    * @param level The expected heading level for the top-level section.
117    * @return The parsed object.
118    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
119    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
120    */
121   protected <T> T parseDocAnything(List<String> lines, ClassMeta<T> eType, Object outer, int level) throws IOException, ParseException {
122      if (eType == null)
123         eType = (ClassMeta<T>) object();
124
125      var swap = (org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.ObjectSwap<T,Object>) eType.getSwap(this);
126      var builder = (org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.BuilderSwap<T,Object>) eType.getBuilderSwap(this);
127      ClassMeta<?> sType;
128      if (builder != null)
129         sType = builder.getBuilderClassMeta(this);
130      else if (swap != null)
131         sType = swap.getSwapClassMeta(this);
132      else
133         sType = eType;
134
135      Object o = null;
136
137      if (sType.isBean()) {
138         o = parseBeanFromDoc(lines, sType, outer, level);
139      } else if (sType.isMap()) {
140         o = parseMapFromDoc(lines, sType, outer, level);
141      } else {
142         // For collections/arrays and simple types, fall back to fragment parsing
143         o = parseAnything(lines, (ClassMeta<T>) sType, outer, null);
144      }
145
146      if (builder != null && o != null)
147         o = builder.build(this, o, eType);
148
149      if (swap != null && o != null)
150         o = unswap(swap, o, eType);
151
152      return (T) o;
153   }
154
155   /**
156    * Parses a bean from Markdown document structure (headings + tables).
157    *
158    * <p>
159    * The top-level heading (title) is ignored. Inline key/value tables at the current heading level
160    * populate simple properties. Sub-headings at {@code level+1} map to nested bean properties.
161    *
162    * @param lines All document lines.
163    * @param sType The bean type.
164    * @param outer The outer object.
165    * @param level The current heading level.
166    * @return The parsed bean.
167    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
168    */
169   @SuppressWarnings({
170      "java:S135" // Multiple continue statements in loop; refactoring would reduce clarity
171   })
172   protected Object parseBeanFromDoc(List<String> lines, ClassMeta<?> sType, Object outer, int level) throws IOException, ParseException {
173      var m = newBeanMap(outer, sType.inner());
174
175      // Segment lines into: top-level content and sub-sections
176      // Sections are delineated by headings at level+1
177      var sections = segmentBySections(lines, level + 1);
178
179      // The "root" section (before any sub-heading) holds the key/value table
180      var rootLines = sections.get("");
181      if (ne(rootLines)) {
182         // Filter out the top-level heading (level)
183         var tableLines = rootLines.stream()
184            .filter(l -> !isHeadingLine(l, level))
185            .toList();
186
187         // Parse the key/value table into the bean
188         var tableRows = tableLines.stream().filter(l -> l.trim().startsWith("|")).toList();
189         if (!tableRows.isEmpty()) {
190            var headers = splitTableRow(tableRows.get(0));
191            var isKeyValue = headers.size() == 2
192               && (headers.get(0).equalsIgnoreCase(CONST_Property) || headers.get(0).equalsIgnoreCase("Key"))
193               && headers.get(1).equalsIgnoreCase(CONST_Value);
194
195            if (isKeyValue) {
196               var dataLines = tableRows.stream().skip(1).filter(l -> !isSeparatorRow(l)).toList();
197               for (var line : dataLines) {
198                  var cells = splitTableRow(line);
199                  if (cells.size() < 2)
200                     continue;
201                  var key = cells.get(0);
202                  var rawVal = cells.get(1);
203                  var pm = m.getPropertyMeta(key);
204                  if (pm != null) {
205                     setCurrentProperty(pm);
206                     var val = parseCellValue(rawVal, (ClassMeta<?>) pm.getBeanInfo(), m.getBean(false));
207                     pm.set(m, key, val);
208                     setCurrentProperty(null);
209                  } else {
210                     onUnknownProperty(key, m, rawVal);
211                  }
212               }
213            }
214         }
215      }
216
217      // Process sub-sections (each maps to a nested bean property)
218      for (var entry : sections.entrySet()) {
219         var sectionName = entry.getKey();
220         if (sectionName.isEmpty())
221            continue; // skip root section already processed
222
223         var sectionLines = entry.getValue();
224         var pm = m.getPropertyMeta(sectionName);
225         if (pm == null)
226            continue; // Skip root, unknown properties, or sections we ignore
227
228         setCurrentProperty(pm);
229         var propCm = (ClassMeta<?>) pm.getBeanInfo();
230
231         // Find first content line to determine if it's a table or list
232         var contentLines = sectionLines.stream()
233            .filter(l -> !isHeadingLine(l, level + 1) && !l.isBlank())
234            .toList();
235
236         Object propVal;
237         if (!contentLines.isEmpty() && contentLines.get(0).trim().startsWith("|")) {
238            // Table content
239            var tableRows = sectionLines.stream().filter(l -> l.trim().startsWith("|")).toList();
240            if (!tableRows.isEmpty()) {
241               var tableHeaders = splitTableRow(tableRows.get(0));
242               var isKeyValue = tableHeaders.size() == 2
243                  && (tableHeaders.get(0).equalsIgnoreCase(CONST_Property) || tableHeaders.get(0).equalsIgnoreCase("Key"))
244                  && tableHeaders.get(1).equalsIgnoreCase(CONST_Value);
245
246               if (isKeyValue && propCm.isBean()) {
247                  // Nested bean via key/value table
248                  propVal = parseDocAnything(sectionLines, (ClassMeta<Object>) propCm, m.getBean(), level + 1);
249               } else {
250                  // Key/value table (non-bean) or multi-column table → list or array
251                  propVal = parseTable(sectionLines, propCm, m.getBean());
252               }
253            } else {
254               propVal = null;
255            }
256         } else if (!contentLines.isEmpty() && isBulletLine(contentLines.get(0))) {
257            // List content
258            propVal = parseBulletList(sectionLines, propCm, m.getBean());
259         } else {
260            propVal = propCm.isBean()
261               ? parseDocAnything(sectionLines, (ClassMeta<Object>) propCm, m.getBean(), level + 1)
262               : parseAnything(sectionLines, (ClassMeta<Object>) propCm, m.getBean(), pm);
263         }
264
265         pm.set(m, sectionName, propVal);
266         setCurrentProperty(null);
267      }
268
269      return m.getBean();
270   }
271
272   /**
273    * Parses a map from Markdown document structure.
274    *
275    * @param lines All document lines.
276    * @param sType The map type.
277    * @param outer The outer object.
278    * @param level The current heading level.
279    * @return The parsed map.
280    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
281    */
282   @SuppressWarnings({
283      "java:S3740" // Raw Map needed for generic map construction from ClassMeta
284   })
285   protected Object parseMapFromDoc(List<String> lines, ClassMeta<?> sType, Object outer, int level) throws IOException, ParseException {
286      // For maps, use the key/value table approach at the root level, then add sub-section maps
287      Map result = sType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Map) sType.newInstance(outer) : newGenericMap();
288      var keyType = sType.getKeyType() != null ? sType.getKeyType() : string();
289      var valueType = sType.getValueType() != null ? sType.getValueType() : object();
290
291      var sections = segmentBySections(lines, level + 1);
292      var rootLines = sections.get("");
293      if (rootLines != null) {
294         var tableRows = rootLines.stream().filter(l -> l.trim().startsWith("|")).toList();
295         if (!tableRows.isEmpty()) {
296            var headers = splitTableRow(tableRows.get(0));
297            var isKeyValue = headers.size() == 2
298               && (headers.get(0).equalsIgnoreCase(CONST_Property) || headers.get(0).equalsIgnoreCase("Key"))
299               && headers.get(1).equalsIgnoreCase(CONST_Value);
300
301            if (isKeyValue) {
302               var dataLines = tableRows.stream().skip(1).filter(l -> !isSeparatorRow(l)).toList();
303               for (var line : dataLines) {
304                  var cells = splitTableRow(line);
305                  if (cells.size() < 2)
306                     continue;
307                  var key = convertAttrToType(result, cells.get(0), keyType);
308                  var val = parseCellValue(cells.get(1), valueType, null);
309                  result.put(key, val);
310               }
311            }
312         }
313      }
314
315      for (var entry : sections.entrySet()) {
316         var sectionName = entry.getKey();
317         if (sectionName.isEmpty())
318            continue;
319         var key = convertAttrToType(result, sectionName, keyType);
320         var val = parseAnything(entry.getValue(), (ClassMeta<Object>) valueType, result, null);
321         result.put(key, val);
322      }
323
324      return result;
325   }
326
327   /**
328    * Segments document lines into sections by heading level.
329    *
330    * <p>
331    * Returns a map where the key is the heading text and the value is the lines in that section.
332    * The empty-string key maps to content before the first sub-heading (root content).
333    *
334    * @param lines All document lines.
335    * @param sectionLevel The heading level that defines section boundaries.
336    * @return Map of section name → section lines.
337    */
338   protected Map<String,List<String>> segmentBySections(List<String> lines, int sectionLevel) {
339      var result = new LinkedHashMap<String,List<String>>();
340      var currentSection = "";
341      var currentLines = new ArrayList<String>();
342      result.put("", currentLines);
343
344      for (var line : lines) {
345         var headingText = getHeadingText(line, sectionLevel);
346         if (headingText != null) {
347            currentSection = headingText;
348            currentLines = new ArrayList<>();
349            result.put(currentSection, currentLines);
350         } else {
351            currentLines.add(line);
352         }
353      }
354
355      return result;
356   }
357
358   /**
359    * Returns the heading text if the given line is a heading at the specified level, else null.
360    *
361    * @param line The input line.
362    * @param level The heading level to check.
363    * @return The heading text or null.
364    */
365   protected String getHeadingText(String line, int level) {
366      var prefix = "#".repeat(level) + " ";
367      var trimmed = line.trim();
368      if (trimmed.startsWith(prefix) && (trimmed.length() == prefix.length() || trimmed.charAt(prefix.length()) != '#'))
369         return trimmed.substring(prefix.length()).trim();
370      return null;
371   }
372
373   /**
374    * Returns whether the given line is a heading at the specified level.
375    *
376    * @param line The input line.
377    * @param level The heading level.
378    * @return Whether it is a heading at that level.
379    */
380   protected boolean isHeadingLine(String line, int level) {
381      return getHeadingText(line, level) != null;
382   }
383
384   /**
385    * Returns whether a line is a bullet list item.
386    *
387    * @param line The input line.
388    * @return Whether it is a bullet item.
389    */
390   protected boolean isBulletLine(String line) {
391      var trimmed = line.trim();
392      return trimmed.startsWith("- ") || trimmed.startsWith("* ") || trimmed.startsWith("+ ");
393   }
394}
